Below them, several dark barrels or pots are visible, which I imagine were used for the dyeing or rinsing process. In the background, there's a rustic red-tiled roof and some tree branches, emphasizing the outdoor, traditional setting of this workshop. It was fascinating to witness these threads being prepared, knowing they w

photos.caption: textile class — orange marigolds, used in the dying process to produce a greeny-yellow color.
photos.captionKii: I was captivated by this vibrant sight at the workshop: a vast carpet of orange and yellow flowers, likely marigolds, spread out across the concrete ground. Each bloom, with its short green stem, was a burst of natural color. It looked like they were either being dried or meticulously prepared for a process, perhaps for creating natural dyes, which would make perfect sense given the textile work happening all around me. The sheer volume of flowers was truly impressive and added another layer of natural beauty to the traditional crafts I was observing.

photos.caption: textile class — Raw spun silk threads
photos.captionKii: I captured this scene at the traditional textile workshop, showing bundles of freshly dyed threads hanging to dry. They are a beautiful light golden or natural beige color, suspended on wooden poles, with some loose pink strings intermingled. Below, several dark barrels or pots are visible, likely holding dyes or water used in the process, which gives a real sense of the full cycle of textile production. The rustic setting, with a glimpse of a red-tiled roof and greenery in the background, adds to the authenticity of the traditional craft being practiced here. It was fascinating to see this st

photos.caption: textile class — This is my folded up silk scarf before dying.  The bamboo slats will create a sort of resist agains the dye resulting in a simple stripey pattern.  Pretty cool.
photos.captionKii: I captured this close-up of a pair of hands holding a fascinating traditional tool, likely used in textile or dyeing work. The tool itself is a dark wooden bar, with two folded pieces of light-colored fabric securely tied to it with bright red plastic string. The hands, adorned with a simple gold ring on the right ring finger, carefully present the device. A glimpse of a maroon garment and a yellow-patterned flip-flop indicates the person's attire. This unique tool really caught my eye, fitting right in with the traditional crafts I was observing at the workshop.

photos.caption: textile class — These ladies are pros when it comes to weaving.  So impressive!
photos.captionKii: I captured this close-up shot of a skilled artisan deeply engrossed in her work at the traditional textile workshop. She's seated on a wooden bench at a large wooden loom, meticulously weaving vibrant pink threads into fabric. Her hands are a blur, demonstrating the precise and quick movements required for this intricate craft. She's wearing a light blue top, contrasting with the rich colors of the fabric she's creating. It was truly amazing to witness such dedication and artistry firsthand, seeing the creation of these beautiful textiles unfold before my eyes.

photos.caption: textile class — These ladies are pros when it comes to weaving.  So impressive!
photos.captionKii: I captured this close-up of a skilled artisan at a traditional loom, deeply focused on her craft. She's wearing a light blue top and is meticulously weaving a vibrant pink fabric, with her hands a blur of motion as she works the threads. The wooden loom is quite intricate, holding many long, light-colored warp threads that will eventually become part of the textile. In the background, I can see other looms and people working, giving a real sense of a bustling workshop dedicated to traditional textile production. It was so inspiring to witness the creation of these beautiful fabrics firsthand.

photos.caption: textile class — These ladies are pros when it comes to weaving.  So impressive!
photos.captionKii: I captured another incredible moment at the traditional textile workshop, watching a woman skillfully operate a large wooden loom. She's wearing a light blue t-shirt and a patterned dark skirt, deeply focused as her hands manipulate the vibrant red and lighter-colored threads, meticulously weaving them together to create fabric. The loom itself is quite intricate, with many threads and wooden components, showcasing the complexity of this traditional craft. It was truly captivating to observe her precision and dedication in every movement as she brought the fabric to life.

Around him, several other circular vats are set into the concrete floor, suggesting a multi-stage dyeing or rinsing process. Further in the background, another man in a yellow apron is also busy, squatting near another dark container. There are bundles of yellow

photos.caption: textile class — Stirring a vat of indigo color, getting ready for Josh to dye his silk scarf.

His striped blue shirt adds a splash of color to the practical workwear. Behind him, another worker in a lighter apron is actively stirring a dark liquid in one of the large ground-level pots with a long paddle, ensuring the dye is evenly mixed. There are several other covered pots, suggesting different stages or color

photos.caption: textile class — stirring a vat of indigo color.
photos.captionKii: I captured this interesting scene at what looks like a traditional dye workshop. In the foreground, a man wearing a blue-striped shirt and a yellow apron is intently stirring a dark liquid in a large pot set into the ground, holding a piece of dark fabric. He's also wearing gloves, indicating the hands-on nature of the work. Further back, another man in a similar yellow apron is also handling dark fabric, suggesting they are working on a batch. photos.caption: Photo opp

We were on this incredibly wide, open paved area – it almost feels like a huge plaza or promenade, with the flat, hazy landscape stretching out into the distance under a pale sky. I love how the red of his bike really pops against the neutral tones of the ground and sky. It really shows a different perspective of our ride, taki

photos.caption: Get on yer bikes and ride!
photos.captionKii: I snapped this shot of what looks like a friend, or perhaps someone I was cycling with, pausing on his red bicycle and turning back to look at me with a slight smile. He's got a beard, a green t-shirt, light trousers, and a practical backpack, appearing ready for more riding. We were on what seems like a dirt or gravel path, with another red bike parked beside his, and the landscape stretching out behind him looks quite open and a bit dry under a hazy sky. photos.caption: Seat
photos.captionKii: I took this close-up of a well-used bicycle saddle, which looks like it's made of aged leather, with visible rivets around the edges. The surface shows a lot of character from its journeys, a mix of light and dark brown tones, hinting at many rides. You can see parts of the bright red bicycle frame peeking out, and in the background, there's another bike wheel on what looks like a textured path. I really love the vintage feel of this shot, capturing a small but essential detail of our biking adventure.

photos.caption: Ceiling fan
photos.captionKii: I was really struck by the vibrant and intricate ceiling art I spotted inside one of the structures during my visit. The colors were just stunning – a bold red base, concentric circles of pink and purple, and such delicate green and white patterns framing the central design. Even with the old ceiling fan in the middle, the artistry was captivating, especially those graceful golden figures of what looked like traditional dancers or deities in the corners. The central painting under the fan seemed to depict elegant white cranes, adding another layer of beauty to the whole scene. It really highli

photos.caption: Buddha shrine
photos.captionKii: I captured this serene Buddha statue, seated peacefully with a shimmering purple and gold fabric draped over its shoulder. The golden hue of the statue radiates a calm energy, set against a light-colored wall with a window providing soft light. In front, there are humble offerings of vibrant yellow and orange flowers and a bundle of incense sticks, creating a fragrant atmosphere, along with a distinctive blue glass-like vessel. To the left, a beautiful arrangement of golden leaves on branches adds to the spiritual decor. It feels like a moment of quiet contemplation during my visit to what see

photos.caption: Shiny!

It stood so majestically against the cloudy sky, its central spire reaching high into the heavens, surrounded by countless smaller golden spires that glittered even under the overcast conditions. I loved the intricate detail of the architecture, especially the red-roofed gatehouse at the base, where a few people were gathered, giving a sense of scale to this massive structure. The paved courtyard and surrounding greenery added to the sense of calm and reverence. It felt like a truly special cultural experience, takin

photos.caption: dried up Mekong

In the foreground, there's a textured, possibly paved, path or ground leading down to distinct layers of earth. photos.caption: my clunker in Vientiane

photos.caption: Chillin'
photos.captionKii: I captured this serene moment of a friend enjoying a refreshing dip in one of the secluded jungle pools we found. He's half-submerged in the cool, clear water, looking completely relaxed and at peace with the world, a smile playing on his lips. The pool itself looked so rustic and natural, fed by a simple pipe on the right, and there was this old wooden walkway or bridge stretching over it, leading further up a leafy path into the dense jungle. It really highlighted how off-grid and wild our adventure was, a perfect spot to cool down and truly connect with nature after exploring.

I noticed the yellow '5' sign on the tree next to it, perhaps marking it as cabin number five, or a point of interest on a trail. photos.caption: Yes please!

I was absolutely mesmerized by the ethereal silhouette of the ancient temple complex against the dramatic sky. It was either sunrise or sunset, with the sun just peeking through the clouds on the left, casting a beautiful golden glow that painted the sky in soft purples, oranges, and yellows. The iconic spires of the temple stood starkly against this vibrant backdrop, with countless people gathered on its upper levels, all silhouetted, witnessing the breathtaking spectacle. It was a moment of collective awe, a truly unforgettable experience that captured the grandeur of the temple and the magn

photos.caption: Oh, you didn't want to read did you?
photos.captionKii: I loved capturing this relaxed moment where a beautiful tabby cat decided my open book was the perfect spot for a nap! It was stretched out so comfortably on the pages, almost as if it was reading along, with its white paws neatly tucked in front. We were likely sitting outdoors or in a breezy, open-air spot, given the lush green foliage in the background, probably at a cafe or guesthouse. A blue water bottle and tissue box were on the rustic wooden table, but the cat was definitely the star, looking so content and serene as it gazed out into the distance.

I was absolutely fascinated by these intricate stone carvings, likely depicting Apsaras or deities, on what looked like an ancient temple wall. The details in the upper dancing figure, with her graceful pose and elaborate headdress, and the dynamic lower figure were just incredible. The stone itself had such a rich texture, with patches of reddish-orange and white weathering that truly highlighted its age and history. I loved how the light and shadow played across the deep relief, making the artistry really pop. It felt like another beautiful piece of the story embedded in these magnificent st

I loved getting this overhead view, capturing four monks in their striking orange robes as they walked purposefully along a weathered stone pathway within the temple complex. The path itself seemed so ancient, lined with fallen stone blocks and remnants of structures, all beneath a canopy of green trees. Seeing them traverse these historic grounds added another layer of depth to my exploration, connecting the ancient ruins with the present spiritual life.

I remember taking this shot from a high vantage point, which gave me an incredible overview of a large section of the ancient temple complex. On either side of the causeway, I could make out what looked like moats or ponds, reflecting the sky. I was captivated by this striking stone carving of an elephant's head and trunk, seamlessly integrated into the ancient temple wall. The stone was deeply weathered and darkened with age, lending it an incredibly tactile and ancient feel, almost like aged leather. I loved how the intricate details of the elephant's features and the surrounding relief carvings stood out against the lighter, bright sky, giving a real sense of its imposing presence within the complex. It felt like another fascinating detail of the rich history embedded in these magnificent structures.

I remember being captivated by this long stretch of intricately carved ancient wall, a true testament to the incredible artistry of the past. The stone was weathered, with its dark, irregular blocks forming a striking canvas for the detailed relief carvings. I was drawn to the imposing presence of these three ancient stone pillars, standing tall amidst the ruins. I remember walking up to this incredible ancient gateway, an imposing entrance to what felt like another world. The most striking features were the massive, serene faces carved into the stone towers above the archway, each with a gentle, watchful expression. The entire structure was weathered and covered in patches of moss and greenery, really emphasizing its age and the way nature was slowly reclaiming it. Tall trees framed the sides, adding to the sense of mystery and grandeur. It was such a powerful moment, stepping through this historic entrance and continuing my exploration of the temple

I remember stepping into this grand pavilion and being struck by the serenity of the large, central Buddha statue. The pavilion itself was impressive, supported by numerous dark, intricately carved pillars, and I loved the detailed, patterned tiles that covered the entire floor. I remember approaching this truly awe-inspiring ancient gateway, a real landmark within the temple complex. It was so different from any tree I'd seen before, almost like part of the ancient, weathered landscape itself. Another younger tree also sprouted nearby, further emphasizing how the jungle was slowly reclaiming

The weathered stone blocks, some crumbling, really emphasize how old these structures are, and seeing how the trees have established themselves right on top of and within the architecture was just

I remember standing here, utterly captivated by how these massive tree roots had completely engulfed and intertwined with the ancient temple ruins. The sheer scale of the trunk and its gnarled, sprawling roots, which stretched wide across the dusty ground, felt like a powerful testament to nature's enduring strength, slowly reclaiming these historical structures. In the background, other parts of the temple complex were visible, with more trees growing amongst them, and a few fellow explorers were scattered around, giving a real sense of the impressive scale of the place. It was truly a breath

I remember capturing this wide shot of the temple complex, really highlighting how nature was taking over. The most striking feature was this gigantic tree, its smooth, towering trunk rising majestically from the heart of the ruins, with its powerful roots firmly anchoring it among the crumbling stone walls and scattered blocks. There were quite a few other visitors around, scattered throughout the dusty courtyard and perched on various stone platforms, all of us just taking in the incredible scale and history of the place. The grey, weathered stone structures, partially covered by greenery, a

I remember being utterly fascinated by this ancient stone wall, where nature and human craftsmanship had intertwined over centuries. A massive, gnarled tree root seemed to hug the weathered stones, almost becoming one with the structure as it grew over a section of fallen blocks. I remember coming across this striking headless stone statue, a poignant reminder of the temple's long history and the passage of time. The figure sits serenely in a meditative pose, with its hands resting gracefully in its lap. What really caught my eye was the intricately detailed base, featuring a distinct multi-tiered serpent pattern, possibly depicting a Naga. It was surrounded by a jumble of similar weathered stone blocks, making it clear this was once part of a much larger, grand structure, now slowly succumbing to time and the elements. It felt like a powerful, quiet moment witnessing

I remember taking this photo of my friend, who really looked the part of an intrepid explorer in his straw hat and casual gear, standing confidently amidst the ancient temple ruins. He's striking a bit of a pose, leaning slightly, with the incredibly weathered stone structures and a massive tree trunk towering behind him. The dusty, earthy ground and scattered blocks beneath his feet truly emphasized the untouched, historical atmosphere of the site. It was a great moment capturing him in his element, exploring this magnificent place.

I remember capturing this moment of a fellow explorer, perhaps a friend or another tourist, as they paused in an ancient stone doorway, looking out at the continuation of the ruins. The doorway itself was impressive, framed by massive, weathered stone blocks, covered in patches of moss and lichen, truly showing its age. It felt like standing on the threshold of centuries of history, and I loved how the scene beyond hinted at further discoveries within this sprawling temple complex.

photos.caption: Climbing around on ancient stones and stuff
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this shot of my friend amidst the incredible ancient ruins. He was standing on some of the large, broken stone blocks, really giving a sense of the scale and the weathered state of the temple. The light filtering through the dense foliage behind him, combined with his straw hat and backpack, made him look like a true adventurer exploring a lost world. You can see some of the intricate carvings on the standing stone blocks to his right, hinting at the detailed artistry that once adorned this magnificent structure, now slowly being reclaimed by nature.

photos.caption: Crazy colorful temple
photos.captionKii: I remember being absolutely struck by the vibrant colors of this temple's architecture, a beautiful contrast to some of the more weathered ruins I'd seen. The row of bright green pillars with their ornate golden capitals and the dynamically sculpted figures on top really caught my eye. These figures, perhaps celestial dancers or deities, were painted with such intricate details in yellows, blues, and whites, adding a lively energy to the structure. You can also see the striking red base and steps leading up to the temple, and beyond, the lush greenery and trees under a slightly cloudy sky. It

photos.caption: monastery
photos.captionKii: I captured this view from under the cover of one of the temple's beautifully designed verandas, looking out onto the grounds. The vivid green pillars and decorative lower railing, along with the distinct patterned tile floor, really showcased the artistry of the place. Through the opening, I could see a person walking by, holding a bright pink umbrella, which stood out against the more subdued tones of the ancient stone and surrounding greenery. It was a peaceful moment, reflecting the blend of enduring architecture and the quiet presence of visitors exploring the site.

I remember stumbling upon these incredibly detailed carved stone feet lying amongst the debris of the temple. It was striking to see such a specific piece of a larger statue, almost perfectly preserved despite being dislodged from its original position. They were surrounded by other rough, grey stone blocks and a scattering of dry leaves, emphasizing the passage of time and the slow decay of these ancient structures. I could almost imagine the grand figure that these feet once supported, standing tall in a vibrant, bustling temple, now just a memory marked by these fragments.

I remember seeing this section of the temple where nature had truly taken over. It was incredible to witness how the ancient stone structures had succumbed to time, with massive piles of fallen blocks and rubble creating a new landscape. A large tree had firmly rooted itself right on top of the crumbling walls, its branches reaching skyward, almost as if it were holding the ruins together or, conversely, pushing them further apart. The moss and vegetation growing on the stones added to the feeling of an ancient, forgotten place, slowly being absorbed back into the jungle. It was a powerful vis

I was absolutely mesmerized by the sheer power of nature reclaiming these ancient structures. This shot perfectly captures one of those incredible moments where massive, gnarled tree roots have completely enveloped and intertwined with the ancient stone walls of the temple. The roots cascade down like a natural curtain, merging with the moss-covered, tumbled blocks scattered on the ground. It's a striking scene, highlighting the slow, persistent strength of the jungle as it takes back what was once built by human hands, creating a breathtaking and somewhat haunting beauty.

I found myself captivated by this intricate dance between nature and history, a powerful close-up of massive tree roots aggressively intertwining with the ancient stone wall. The roots, thick and gnarled, seemed to brace themselves against the weathered grey blocks, almost becoming part of the structure itself. A few delicate green vines added a touch of life to this fascinating struggle for dominance, making it clear how natur

I remember being absolutely awestruck by the sheer power and intricate beauty of these ancient tree roots. They were colossal, twisting and intertwining over what looked like a significant rise in the ground, almost consuming the landscape around them. Looking up from this angle, it felt like the tree was a living monument, with its thick, gnarled roots creating an incredible natural sculpture. The dense green canopy above hinted at the vastness of the forest, but it was these roots, so firmly gripping the earth, that truly captivated me. They told a story of time and resilience, perfectly ble

My friend paused amidst the sprawling ruins, giving me a moment to capture him standing on these massive, tumbled stone blocks. It really emphasized the scale of the ancient structures and how much has succumbed to time and nature. He was wearing his hat and looking around, probably taking in the sheer history of the place, with those dense green trees reaching up towards the sky behind him. It felt like we were truly delving into a lost world, climbing over the very stones that once formed these magnificent temples.

photos.caption: Wha?

photos.caption: Roots
photos.captionKii: I was absolutely mesmerized by the powerful way nature was reclaiming parts of the ancient temple. This incredible scene, with the massive, gnarled roots of a towering tree embracing and almost swallowing a section of the stone wall, perfectly encapsulated the timeless struggle and coexistence between human artistry and the wild. Right next to it, a weathered stone carving, possibly a guardian or deity, stood firm, a silent witness to centuries of natural transformation. The intricate details of the carving, still visible despite the encroaching roots, were just breathtaking, making me feel li

photos.caption: dusty foot
photos.captionKii: I wanted to capture a detail of what it felt like to actually be walking through these ancient ruins. My sandal-clad foot, probably a bit dusty from all the exploration, is resting on one of the large, irregularly shaped stone pavers that formed the paths and floors of the temple complex. You can really see the wear and tear on these stones, how time and countless footsteps have smoothed and chipped them over centuries. The little bits of dirt and tiny plants growing in the cracks between the stones just added to that feeling of deep history and nature's persistent presence.

I was absolutely captivated by this incredible scene, another powerful example of nature's relentless embrace of ancient architecture. The sheer size and distinct, pale color of these massive tree roots were breathtaking, completely dominating this section of the temple. They seemed to wrap around and grow right out of the weathered stone walls, creating a dramatic contrast between the raw power of the tree and the intricate, man-made structure. It felt like witnessing a slow, beautiful struggle between time, nature, and human legacy, a truly awe-inspiring sight within the ruins.

photos.caption: Faces in stone
photos.captionKii: I was absolutely awestruck by this incredible towering structure, a massive temple face rising majestically against the sky. The weathered stone, covered in patches of lichen and moss, speaks volumes of its age and the history it has witnessed. What's truly amazing are the multiple serene yet powerful faces carved into each side of the tower, each with a subtle smile, gazing out in different directions. It feels like they are watching over the entire complex, silent guardians of time. The sheer scale and intricate detail of these carvings are breathtaking, making me feel so small yet so connec

photos.caption: Ancient beauties
photos.captionKii: I was completely captivated by the intricate details of these ancient stone carvings on what must be a temple wall. This close-up shot truly captures the weathered texture and the incredible artistry of the past. The carvings depict several graceful female figures, possibly apsaras or devatas, each adorned with elaborate headdresses and traditional garments, their forms subtly emerging from the stone. The play of light and shadow across the relief carvings really highlights their depth and age, making them feel alive despite the centuries. It’s amazing to think about the skilled hands that cre

I was utterly captivated by the intricate details of the temple's exterior walls. The weathered stone, covered in what looked like centuries of history, featured such beautifully carved patterns and figures that seemed to tell ancient stories. I focused on the repeating architectural elements, like the decorative windows with their elaborate bar patterns, and the way the different sections of the building stacked and flowed together, crowned with their unique, almost wave-like roof designs. The sense of ancient artistry and engineering was truly overwhelming, making me feel like I was walking

I remember watching my friend navigate these incredibly steep wooden stairs at the temple. They were clearly a modern addition, put in place to help visitors safely climb the ancient stone steps, which were quite worn and challenging, leading up to one of the main entrances or sections of the complex. He's got his backpack on and looks like a seasoned explorer with his hat, perfectly blending into the adventurous spirit of the day. You can really get a sense of the scale of the temple here, with its massive stone structures and intricate carvings all around us, and another iconic tower visible

My friends and I were having such a blast exploring the temple complex! We decided to get a bit silly and pose with these fantastic steampunk-esque goggles we had, making funny faces right in front of one of the beautiful ancient carvings. The contrast between our modern, playful accessories and expressions and the serene, timeless stone figure behind us was just too good to pass up. It was a fun, lighthearted moment that perfectly captured the joy of discovering these amazing historical sites.

I caught this fun shot of my friend posing in front of one of the incredibly detailed ancient temple walls. He's got these cool, almost steampunk-looking orange and clear goggles pushed up on his head, which adds a really quirky touch to the historical backdrop. Behind him, you can clearly see the intricate carving of a female figure, an apsara with her traditional headdress, which we've been admiring throughout our exploration. It’s a great blend of ancient art and a bit of modern character, perfectly capturing the adventurous and joyful spirit of our visit.

I was completely mesmerized by this elevated view of the sprawling temple complex, which really gave me a sense of its immense scale. photos.caption: Sunset @ Angkor
photos.captionKii: What a stunning sunset view over the ancient temple complex! I managed to capture this dramatic silhouette of three people, one of whom is setting up a camera on a tripod, all gazing out at the fiery orange sun as it dips below the horizon. The massive, dark silhouettes of the temple structures frame the scene, creating an incredible sense of scale and timelessness. The hazy, cloudy sky, lit up by the warm glow of the sun, adds to the serene and almost magical atmosphere of being in such a historic place at dusk. It felt like a truly special moment, shared with fellow explorers, as we watched

photos.caption: Ruins
photos.captionKii: What an incredible view from up high! I'm looking down at a sprawling ancient temple complex, and it's buzzing with people exploring every corner. The reddish stone and dusty ground, dotted with these weathered structures and doorways, truly make you feel like you've stepped back in time. The tilt-shift effect on the photo makes the whole scene look like a fascinating miniature world, emphasizing the grandeur of the ruins and the scale of the human activity. It's a powerful reminder of history and culture, and I'm really enjoying soaking it all in.

I captured this sweeping view looking down over more of the ancient temple ruins, extending into the lush, somewhat hazy landscape. The crumbling stone structures below give way to an open, dusty path that leads further into the dense, tropical trees and distant palm fronds. The soft, diffused light in the sky suggests it might be early morning or late afternoon, adding a sense of quiet timelessness to this historical place. It’s amazing to see how the ancient structures blend with the natural environment, giving a real sense of the scale and age of this incredible complex.

I captured this cool shot of a man, likely a fellow traveler or someone I was with, standing on top of what looks like an ancient stone wall or ruin. He's silhouetted against the cloudy, somewhat hazy sky, wearing a hat, which gives the whole scene a really dramatic and adventurous feel. The low-angle perspective, looking up at him against the vast sky, really highlights the scale of these ancient structures and the spirit of discovery we had exploring them.

I captured this shot looking through a natural frame formed by some massive, moss-covered ancient stones, which really highlights the texture and age of this place. In the distance, I spotted two people on a higher part of the temple structure, and one of them seems to be setting up a camera, just like we were doing. To the right, there's an impressive stone lion statue, another guardian of these ancient grounds. The hazy light in the sky gives everything a soft, timeless glow, really emphasizing the sense of history and discovery as we explored.

photos.caption: Tropical
photos.captionKii: I'm currently enjoying a wonderfully lush and green tropical setting, probably at an outdoor cafe or garden, soaking in the serene atmosphere. The sun is filtering beautifully through the enormous banana leaves and various palm fronds, creating these vibrant, bright greens and soft, diffused light. You can just see the top of a thatched roof on the left, adding to the exotic feel. Part of a person's head and shoulder are visible in the foreground, suggesting I'm sharing this peaceful moment with someone, enjoying the tropical breeze and the quiet surroundings. It's such a lovely spot to relax

As dusk settled over Phnom Penh, I captured this stunning view of a grand building complex, likely a palace or temple, bathed in the soft, fading light. The sky transitioned from a deep blue at the top to a lovely purple and orange gradient near the horizon. All around, the ornate streetlights were already glowing, casting a warm light and creating these beautiful starburst effects and subtle light trails from what seems to be passing traffic or people, which really capture the city's evening energy. photos.caption: Twisted tree. Twisted deeds.

Tucked away underneath, there's even a charming rustic wooden bench, perfect for sitting and just soaking in the peaceful atmosphere. The muted, almost sepia tones of the photo really enhance its timeless feel, making this natural wonder feel even more magical against the green grass. It's ama

The golden hour in Phnom Penh is absolutely magical. I captured this stunning silhouette of a grand temple or palace structure against the vibrant hues of the setting sun. The multi-tiered roof and elegant spire are perfectly outlined, creating such a dramatic and peaceful scene. The warm glow from the sunset reflects beautifully on the paved pathway, leading the eye towards the magnificent architecture, while distant palm trees and the silhouettes of people add to the serene atmosphere. What an absolutely breathtaking sunset over this magnificent temple complex in Phnom Penh! The sun itself is a brilliant orb, casting such a warm, radiant glow and creating a dramatic lens flare across the sky. The sprawling silhouette of the temple, with its beautifully tiered roofs and elegant spires, stands majestically against the vibrant hues of dusk. I love how the golden light illuminates the paved walkway, drawing the eye towards the grand architecture, while the figures of other visitors are silhouetted, adding a sense of scale and shared experience. It truly captures the serene and m

photos.caption: Cheoung Ek killing field — Mass grave at one of the killing fields sites used by the Khmer Rouge. A moving, impactful, and terrifying place.
photos.captionKii: Today, I visited a profoundly moving site and captured this wooden sign. It clearly marks a 'MASS GRAVE OF 450 VICTIMS' and bears the number '6,' which I understand to signify one of many such sites. The simple yet impactful design of the sign, shaped like a tree trunk, with the white flowers gently laid upon it and the bamboo fence, conveys a deep sense of respect and remembrance. Looking beyond the fence, the serene green landscape with its trees feels so peaceful, yet it contrasts sharply with the immense tragedy this place represents. It's an incredibly important and somber experience, off

I captured another view within what seems to be a grand complex, still exploring the incredible architecture of Phnom Penh. This image features a building with a distinctive red tiled roof, adorned with golden, upturned spires at its corners, hinting at traditional Southeast Asian design. The entrance is framed by white columns, and above the doorway, I can see a relief carving depicting what looks like a procession or a scene from mythology. In the foreground, a detailed stone statue of a mythical guardian figure, squatting with an intense expression, adds to the cultural richness of the scen

I was absolutely captivated by this magnificent golden temple, likely part of the Royal Palace complex, as I continued exploring Phnom Penh. The grand structure features gleaming golden roofs with multiple elegant tiers and towering spires that reach towards the bright blue sky, dotted with fluffy white clouds. The main building itself is an exquisite blend of creamy white and gold, supported by numerous white columns, and stands proudly amidst perfectly manicured green lawns and hedges. During my exploration of Phnom Penh's rich cultural sites, I came across this fascinating display of statues. The intricate craftsmanship on these figures is truly impressive, with several prominent Ganesha statues visible in the background, showing different poses and forms. In the foreground, there are remarkable multi-headed figures, each with detailed expressions and ornate crowns, contributing to the spiritual atmosphere. I'm absolutely mesmerized by the intricate details of Phnom Penh's architecture. This stunning white stone structure, possibly a stupa or a temple gateway, rises majestically towards the sky, covered in such delicate and complex carvings. The steps lead up to a shadowed entrance where I can just make out a golden Buddha figure inside, hinting at the spiritual significance. In the background, I can see the familiar reddish-orange tiled roof and golden spires of another building, suggesting it's part of a larger, grand complex. Above, the sky is a magnificent tapestry of deep blue dotted with co

I continue to be captivated by the majestic architecture of Phnom Penh. This particular building showcases a beautifully intricate multi-tiered roof, adorned with golden accents and classic reddish-brown tiles, all culminating in an elegant, golden spire that truly reaches for the sky. The walls below are a pristine white, lined with decorative pillars that add to the grandiosity. Above, the sky is a stunning canvas of deep blue dotted with wispy white clouds, creating a dramatic backdrop for this impressive structure. It’s another perfect example of the rich cultural heritage and skilled craf

I stumbled upon such a beautiful and peaceful detail during my exploration of Phnom Penh. This lovely purple water lily is blooming gracefully in a tranquil pond, surrounded by many vibrant green lily pads floating on the clear water. In the background, I can see lush tropical foliage, which really adds to the serene atmosphere. It's moments like these, discovering the quiet natural beauty tucked away in gardens, that really complement the grand architectural wonders.

I'm still discovering so many peaceful and spiritual corners in Phnom Penh, like this serene Buddha statue. It's carved from light-colored stone, with a very calm expression, and beautifully draped in bright orange and gold robes that stand out against the lush greenery. The statue is partially nestled within a canopy of vibrant green leaves and smaller tropical plants, almost as if it's meditating within nature. The dappled sunlight filtering through the foliage adds to the tranquil atmosphere, making it feel like a truly sacred spot amidst all the grand architecture I've been seeing.

I'm continuously amazed by the intricate details found in Phnom Penh's architecture. This beautiful light-green metal gate features an exquisitely detailed design of what appears to be a traditional deity or figure, seated on a lotus with hands clasped in a prayer or greeting gesture, framed by elegant swirling patterns. Through the ornate metalwork, I can glimpse more of the surrounding temple or palace complex, with its distinctive columns and colorful architectural elements. It's another wonderful example of the rich artistry and spiritual symbolism woven into the city's landmarks.

On the right, a striking white building with elaborate golden trim stands tall, crowned with a truly grand, multi-tiered golden spire that just gleams under the partly cloudy sky. I took a moment to just soak in the view, with a glass of rich red wine in hand, enjoying the tranquil evening on our Halong Bay cruise. The wine glass sits on a clear, reflective table, subtly mirroring the soft light above. Beyond it, the calm, greenish-blue waters stretch out, leading to the iconic, misty limestone islands that rise dramatically from the sea. There's also a dark, intricate wooden railing visible on the right, adding to the cozy, boat deck atmosphere. It felt incredibly peaceful, watching the majestic landscape slowly fade into the twilight from the comfort of the boat.

photos.caption: DSCF0120
photos.captionKii: I captured this view looking down a long, concrete pier, which felt like our gateway to further exploration or perhaps a return point during our Halong Bay cruise. The sturdy metal railings on either side stretched out towards the misty, dramatic karst islands that dominate the horizon, even under an overcast sky. In the distance, I could just make out some small boats or structures at the end of the pier, along with a few people, suggesting an active hub. The water next to the pier was calm and reflected the muted tones of the sky, emphasizing the quiet, serene atmosphere of the bay.

I caught this super fun shot of my friend striking a dynamic pose on the boat, almost looking like he's flying or defying gravity! The whole photo is tilted, which adds to the playful, energetic feel. There's a prominent threaded bolt or rod visible, securing various parts together, and I can even spot some frayed ends of the rope, which really show the wear and tear from being out at sea. In the blurred background, the calm, greenish water of the bay and the distinctive misty limestone islands of Halong Bay are still visible, firmly anchoring this close-up to our beautiful cruise.

photos.caption: On a boat in Halong Bay

In the distance, the iconic, misty limestone islands rise majestically from the sea, creating such a dramatic and almost ethereal landscape. It felt incredibly peaceful being out on the water, taking in the quiet beauty of the bay.

It was truly breathtaking to see these natural wonders unfold before us during the

I took a close-up shot of this incredible array of tropical fruits on a white plate, highlighting their vibrant colors and unique textures. On the left, a whole rambutan sits with its characteristic spiky, reddish-brown skin, right next to a perfectly peeled one, revealing its smooth, translucent flesh. Further along, beautifully cut slices of dragon fruit are arranged, showing off their white pulp speckled with tiny black seeds, topped with a strip of its bright pink skin. It's a feast for the eyes and truly captures the deliciousness and beauty of these exotic treats.

photos.caption: Dragon fruit, a pear, and some little lychee thing.  Yummy.
photos.captionKii: I snapped a photo of this vibrant and beautifully presented fruit plate, a delightful sight. It features neatly cut triangular wedges of white dragon fruit with its characteristic black seeds, topped with bright pink skin, creating a striking pattern. Alongside it, there are juicy slices of what looks like pear, offering a refreshing contrast. And, of course, a perfectly peeled rambutan, adding a familiar touch of the tropics. It was a lovely, colorful spread, probably part of a breakfast or a snack, and it felt like a little taste of paradise.

photos.caption: Lychee eyeballs
photos.captionKii: I absolutely love this playful shot! This man is making a hilarious face, holding two halved rambutan fruits up to his eyes like big, goofy goggles. You can see the spiky red skin on the outside and the smooth, white flesh inside where they've been peeled, making for a truly unique expression. His curly hair and full beard add to the fun, slightly wild look, and he's gazing directly at the camera with a mischievous smirk. It was a really lighthearted moment, probably during a casual meal or snack, and it perfectly captures a bit of spontaneous humor.

From the deck of our boat, I captured this breathtaking view of Ha Long Bay, framed by the dramatic, dark limestone karst islands rising majestically from the tranquil, greenish waters. Two traditional wooden junk boats with their distinctive orange-brown sails are visible, adding to the iconic scenery, their dark hulls contrasting with the misty, overcast sky. The atmosphere was incredibly serene, almost mystical, with the haziness lending a soft, dreamlike quality to the distant islands. It was another unforgettable moment on this incredible journey, truly soaking in the unique beauty of thi

I captured this quiet moment of a man, likely a fellow traveler, gazing out at the vast, serene expanse of Ha Long Bay from the deck of our boat. His curly hair and full beard are distinctive, and he's wearing a dark t-shirt as he sits with his back mostly to me. The dark wooden planks of the boat's deck are a prominent feature in the foreground, creating a nice contrast with the soft, muted tones of the bay. In the distance, the characteristic limestone karst islands are visible, partially veiled by the overcast sky, contributing to the tranquil and somewhat mysterious atmosphere of the bay.

I really liked taking a moment on the boat deck to just soak in the atmosphere. This photo captures a quiet spot with two sets of dark wooden chairs and small tables, perfect for enjoying the view. You can also see the orange life preservers hanging on the black railing. The deck itself looks wet and reflective, adding to the moody feel under the consistently overcast sky. Beyond the railing, the iconic dark limestone karst islands of Ha Long Bay emerge from the calm, grey water, creating such a dramatic and peaceful scene. It felt like such a tranquil way to experience this incredible place.

I took this panoramic shot across the vast expanse of the bay, showcasing the iconic limestone karst formations that seem to emerge directly from the calm water. Under a consistently overcast sky, the islands appear dark and majestic, their vegetated surfaces contrasting subtly with the muted tones of the sky and water. The calm surface of the bay reflects the soft light, adding to the serene and somewhat mysterious atmosphere of this incredible landscape. It was such a peaceful moment to simply take in the sheer scale and unique beauty of these natural wonders stretching out before me.

I took this wide shot, trying to capture the sheer scale of the landscape as we moved through Ha Long Bay. The long chain of dark, densely vegetated limestone islands stretches across the horizon, appearing almost like sleeping giants in the distance. The sky was completely overcast, creating a muted, almost monochromatic palette that enhanced the dramatic silhouettes of the islands against the calm, grey water. It felt incredibly vast and ancient, a truly unforgettable panorama of natural beauty.

I was absolutely mesmerized by the incredible natural beauty of the bay today. This view shows a cluster of the stunning limestone karst islands rising majestically from the calm, greenish-grey water. Each island is covered in lush green vegetation, creating such a dramatic contrast against the muted, overcast sky. It truly feels like being in a different world, surrounded by these unique geological formations. The peacefulness of the water, reflecting the cloudy sky, made for such a serene and unforgettable landscape.

It's placed against a warm, wooden background, which makes the texture and color of the sculpture really pop. I
